name: Cash Cow
description_short: Louisiana-based lender offering payday loans up to $700 and auto title loans up to $1,400 with no credit check required, serving residents through multiple stores statewide.
description_long:
Cash Cow Louisiana has operated as a consumer lender in the state since approximately 1994, giving it over 30 years in the short-term loan market. Headquartered in Baton Rouge, the company serves Louisiana residents through retail locations across Baton Rouge, New Orleans, Monroe, Lafayette, Alexandria, Kenner, Gretna, Opelousas, Gonzales, Marksville, and other communities statewide. It is licensed as a consumer finance company under Louisiana state regulations. No third-party certifications such as CDFI designation, HUD approval, or NFCC affiliation have been identified.

The company's two core products are payday loans and auto title loans. Payday loans reach up to $700, with in-store amounts typically in the $100–$300 range, and require no credit check. Auto title loans are available up to $1,400 for vehicle owners, with all credit types accepted; borrowers retain possession of their car throughout the repayment period. Cash Cow claims approval in under 30 minutes. Interest rates and APR are not publicly disclosed on the company's website — specific terms require an in-store visit or phone call. Louisiana's short-term consumer lending market typically carries payday loan APRs averaging around 400%, a relevant benchmark for cost comparisons.

Cash Cow Louisiana's key differentiators are its statewide retail footprint, its three-decade presence in the Louisiana subprime lending market, and its no-credit-check policy on payday products. The company provides both an online application portal and an online payment portal at cashcowlouisiana.com, offering some convenience beyond in-store-only transactions. Its geographic coverage across Louisiana's major population centers makes same-day access realistic for a large share of the state's residents. The company holds a 5.0/5 Google rating based on 140 reviews, reflecting a consistent pattern of borrower satisfaction at the point of service.

The most significant limitation is cost opacity — Cash Cow Louisiana does not publish its rates, and Louisiana's regulatory environment permits very high APRs on short-term loans. The Better Business Bureau has assigned the Baton Rouge location an F rating, citing failure to respond to more than 30 consumer complaints, and the company is not BBB accredited. Loan amounts are modest: the $700 payday cap and $1,400 title loan ceiling will not cover larger financial emergencies. These products are best understood as a high-cost last resort for borrowers who have exhausted traditional lending options, not as competitive alternatives to credit unions, CDFIs, or conventional personal loan lenders.
address: 7551 W Judge Perez Dr, Arabi, LA 70032

pricing.guarantee_details: Not applicable. No money-back guarantee exists for loan products. Contact provider for current rate and fee details.

pros: 30+ years operating as a consumer lender in Louisiana since approximately 1994
No credit check required for payday loan applications
All credit types accepted for auto title loans
Borrower keeps vehicle possession throughout the title loan repayment period
Claims approval decisions in under 30 minutes
Multiple store locations across Louisiana including New Orleans metro, Baton Rouge, Lafayette, and Monroe
Online application and payment portal available at cashcowlouisiana.com
cons: Interest rates and APR not publicly disclosed; Louisiana payday market average is approximately 400% APR
BBB F rating at Baton Rouge location due to failure to respond to 30+ consumer complaints
Services restricted to Louisiana residents only
Maximum loan amounts are small — $700 for payday loans, $1,400 for title loans — insufficient for larger emergencies
best_for: Louisiana residents facing an immediate cash shortfall who have poor or no credit history
Vehicle owners in Louisiana needing up to $1,400 quickly and who cannot qualify for traditional financing
Borrowers in Louisiana's underserved communities who have no access to credit unions or bank personal loans
Repeat short-term borrowers in Louisiana who need fast turnaround and in-person service
diagnosis: Cash Cow Louisiana is genuinely suited for Louisiana residents in urgent financial need who have poor or no credit and few conventional borrowing alternatives, particularly those who can leverage a vehicle for a title loan. The central caveat is cost: the company does not disclose its rates publicly, Louisiana permits very high short-term lending APRs, and an F rating from the BBB signals a pattern of unresolved consumer complaints that prospective borrowers should weigh carefully.
services: In-store loan applications at multiple Louisiana locations
Vehicle retention during title loan repayment
